purpose: Severity scoring criteria for the code-reviewer skill. Use these definitions to rate every finding consistently.

critical.label: Critical
critical.definition: A security vulnerability or data-loss risk that can be exploited in production or that causes irreversible harm.
critical.merge_policy: Block merge. Must be fixed before code ships to any environment.
criteria: Remote code execution (RCE) possible
SQL injection or command injection
Authentication bypass
Sensitive data (passwords, PII, secrets) exposed
Privilege escalation
Arbitrary file read/write
Insecure deserialization of untrusted data
examples: User input passed to eval()
SQL query built with string concatenation
Passwords stored as plain text
Hardcoded API key or database credential
pickle.loads() on user-supplied data

high.label: High
high.definition: A likely bug that causes incorrect behavior in normal use, or a security weakness that requires specific conditions to exploit but still poses real risk.
high.merge_policy: Block merge. Fix before shipping to production.
criteria: Logic error that produces incorrect results under normal usage
Null/nil dereference that will crash in known scenarios
Race condition with real concurrency risk
Security weakness that requires unusual but plausible user behavior
Missing error handling on operations that will fail in production
N+1 query on an endpoint called frequently
examples: Off-by-one error in pagination logic
Missing null check on value from external API
MD5 used for password hashing
Missing rate limiting on authentication endpoint
Goroutine leak in request handler

medium.label: Medium
medium.definition: A code quality or maintainability issue that increases the risk of future bugs, makes the code harder to understand, or violates established conventions.
medium.merge_policy: Prefer to fix before merge. Acceptable to merge with a tracked follow-up ticket.
criteria: Function or method is too long (>40 lines) and has multiple responsibilities
Cyclomatic complexity > 10 (deeply nested conditionals)
Magic numbers or hardcoded strings that should be named constants
Duplicated logic that should be extracted
Silent error handling that hides real failures
Missing validation on inputs at a system boundary
examples: 200-line function handling parsing, validation, DB write, and email send
Nested if/else 6 levels deep
if status == 3: (no named constant explaining what status 3 means)
Same 15-line data transformation duplicated in 3 files

low.label: Low
low.definition: A minor style, naming, or convention issue. No functional impact but reduces readability or deviates from team/language standards.
low.merge_policy: Merge is fine. Address when convenient or batch with related changes.
low.response_time: Fix when touching the code again
criteria: Variable or function name is not descriptive
Inconsistent code style (spacing, casing, quote style)
Dead code or unused imports
Commented-out code left in production
Missing docstring or comment on non-obvious logic
Slight deviation from idiomatic language style
examples: Variable named 'data' or 'temp' with no context
Mix of single and double quotes in Python file
import os at top of file but os is never used
# old implementation\n# result = get_old_data() left in file

suggestion.label: Suggestion
suggestion.definition: A non-blocking improvement, alternative approach, or future consideration. Not a problem — just a potentially better way.
suggestion.merge_policy: Always fine to merge. Apply at author's discretion.
criteria: A more idiomatic or concise way to express the same logic
A library or built-in that would replace custom implementation
An opportunity to improve testability
A performance optimization that may not yet be needed
A pattern that scales better for anticipated growth
examples: list comprehension instead of for loop + .append()
dataclasses or Pydantic model instead of dict for structured data
Context manager (with statement) for resource cleanup
Consider adding an index if this query runs frequently

scoring_rules.group_similar: If 3+ findings share the same root cause (e.g., 4 instances of the same SQL injection pattern), group them into one finding with all locations listed.
scoring_rules.no_invented_findings: If a category has no issues, explicitly state 'No issues found in this category' rather than omitting it.

context_sensitivity.internal_script: Lower severity for issues unlikely to receive external input (e.g., a one-time data migration script).
context_sensitivity.public_api: Raise severity for any issue where user-controlled data reaches sensitive operations.
context_sensitivity.authentication_code: Any security finding in authentication/authorization code defaults to Critical.
